subject: Computer Networking
title: 5G: Speed, Latency, and Capacity
description: A 60-second lesson on 5G: Speed, Latency, and Capacity.

hook.text: 5G isn't just faster 4G. It's three networks in one: high speed, massive connectivity, and ultra-low latency.

buildup.text: Enhanced mobile broadband delivers gigabit speeds. Massive IoT connects millions of sensors per square kilometer.

discovery.text: Ultra-reliable low latency targets 1 ms delay. That enables remote surgery and autonomous vehicle communication.

twist.text: Millimeter wave 5G is blazing fast but barely penetrates walls. Sub-6 GHz 5G has better range but lower peak speeds.

climax.text: Carriers blend both. Mmwave for stadiums and downtowns, sub-6 for broad coverage. It's a layered strategy.

punchline.text: 5G serves three very different needs with one standard.

quiz.question: What are the three service categories of 5G?
options: Enhanced broadband, massive IoT, and ultra-reliable low latency
